channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: San Fermin Bull Run Festival Concludes with Two Injuries
item.description: The San Fermin bull run festival in Pamplona, Spain, concluded with two individuals sustaining serious injuries after being gored by a bull. The incident occurred during the final day of the annual ev...

item.title: US-Iran Conflict Escalates Over Control of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The prolonged conflict between the United States and Iran has intensified over control of the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz. US President Donald Trump ended a ceasefire, reimposed sanctions on ...

item.title: Pakistan Condemns Houthi Attacks on Saudi Arabia, Supports KSA Security
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan has strongly condemned the recent attacks on Saudi Arabia, emphasizing that such acts threaten regional peace and stability. He reiterated Islamabad's unwaver...

item.title: Clark Develops Comprehensive Airport City Infrastructure
item.description: The article discusses Clark's ambitious project to establish its facility as the first and only airport city in the region. This initiative aims to integrate air transport with urban development, crea...

item.title: Astronomers Detect Sugar in Galactic Gas Cloud, Suggesting Prebiotic Ingredients
item.description: Astronomers have detected erythrulose, a sugar found in raspberries and self-tanners, in a gas cloud near the center of the Milky Way. While not essential for life itself, the molecule can convert int...

item.title: PCEA Mai a Ihii Booth High School Closure Due to Principal's Arrest and Safety Concerns
item.description: PCEA Mai a Ihii Booth High School in Kikuyu, Kiambu County, has been closed indefinitely following safety concerns linked to the arrest of its principal and board chairperson. The closure was announce...

item.title: Argentina's World Cup semifinal clash with England faces refereeing controversy scrutiny
item.description: Argentina's progression to the World Cup semifinals against England has sparked renewed debate over refereeing decisions, with critics accusing the tournament of favoring Lionel Messi's team. The cont...

item.title: NBI finds no threats against Sara Duterte during impeachment trial
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) testified during Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial that no relevant threats were found against her life. NBI Bangsamoro chief Jeremy Lotoc stat...

item.title: Governor Buni's Full Implementation of ASUU Agreement to Enhance Education and Staff Welfare at Yobe State University
item.description: The Vice-Chancellor of Yobe State University (YSU), Professor Muhammad Bashir Tahir, has praised Governor Mai Mala Buni for approving the full implementation of the 2025 Federal Government/Academic St...

item.title: Malaysia probes tech commune led by ex-Coinbase exec over immigration concerns
item.description: Malaysia's Home Affairs Ministry is investigating a co-living community for digital nomads founded by former Coinbase executive Balaji Srinivasan after allegations surfaced that the Network School hos...

item.title: Boyfriend of Knysna Mother Faces Trial After Alleged Murder and Google Searches
item.description: The case of Velia Strydom, a Knysna mother who was allegedly strangled to death by her boyfriend Mervyn Bock, is set for trial in the High Court on August 5. Bock, 40, is accused of murdering Strydom ...

item.title: Citizen TV Actress Nurah Yusuf Celebrates Traditional Pre-Wedding Ceremony with Colleagues and Family
item.description: Citizen TV actress Nurah Yusuf recently celebrated a traditional pre-wedding ceremony attended by colleagues, family, and friends. The event was marked by cultural rituals, communal cheering, and gift...

item.title: Malawian Nationals Stranded in Johannesburg Seek Repatriation Amid Xenophobia Crisis
item.description: Over 1,000 Malawians remain stranded outside the Malawian consulate in Johannesburg, waiting for transportation back to their home country after fleeing anti-immigrant violence in South Africa. The cr...

item.title: England and Argentina face off in World Cup semi-final after 40-year rivalry
item.description: England and Argentina are set to meet again in the FIFA 2026 World Cup semi-finals, reigniting one of football's most iconic rivalries. The clash, held in Atlanta, marks the sixth World Cup meeting be...

item.title: Canada Allocates $71 Million to Expand Youth Mental Health Services in Quebec
item.description: The federal government has announced a $71 million investment over four years to expand youth mental health services in Quebec, addressing growing concerns about young people's mental health challenge...

item.title: NCP leader denies internal conflict, rejects legal challenge to Sunetra Pawar's leadership
item.description: Praful Patel, national working president of the Nationalist Congress Party (NCP), has dismissed claims of internal rifts within the party, stating that a legal challenge by national secretary Sachchid...

item.title: SDP’s Adebayo Criticizes Tinubu and Shettima’s Leadership Style as Un-Islamic
item.description: The presidential candidate of the Social Democratic Party (SDP), Adewole Adebayo, has criticized President Bola Tinubu and Vice President Kashim Shettima, arguing their leadership style contradicts Is...

item.title: African Nations' Earnings from FIFA World Cup 2026 Breakdown
item.description: The article details how African countries earned KSh 17.4 billion from the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with Morocco and Egypt securing the highest payouts. Nine African teams reached the knockout stages, wit...

item.title: LPG Powers Cold Chain Infrastructure for Tanzania’s Fisheries Sector
item.description: The article discusses how liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) is critical to Tanzania's fisheries sector, which supports millions of people. With government investment in Kilwa Masoko Fishing Port, the focu...

item.title: Canadian Ships Await Departure from Gulf After Five Months
item.description: The article discusses the situation of two Canadian naval vessels that have been stationed in the Gulf for nearly five months and are now eager to depart. The ships, which have been under observation ...
